item.title: 10 REM"_(C2SLFF4
item.description: Wherein we chase down a BASIC retrocomputing question that's bugged me for far too long.

item.title: On Making
item.description: Out of all the things to love and hate with AI, this is what I miss.

item.title: Rust RPN Calculator
item.description: Another Rust rabbit hole digging into some RPN calculator code.

item.title: Exploring Rust Traits
item.description: Just randomly rabbit-holing down into how traits work in Rust.

item.title: Using Virtual Environments in Python
item.description: A short how-to with just the basics to get going.

item.title: Stop Using DRM on your Books
item.description: Why not to protect your books with DRM.

item.title: Adding Mastodon Comments to your Blog
item.description: Comments powered by Mastodon? Yes, please!

item.title: How Do You Prove Humanity?
item.description: Reflections on the new wave of AI.

item.title: On the Importance of Hard Work
item.description: A historical retrogaming morality tale.

item.title: Using Gamepads and Joysticks in JavaScript
item.description: Let's get some games written!

item.title: Websockets: Writing the Server
item.description: Part 3 in the series: writing the server in NodeJS.

item.title: Websockets: Writing the Client
item.description: Part 2 in the series: writing the client-side JavaScript.

item.title: Intro to WebSockets
item.description: An overview of WebSockets, with a goal of writing a simple WebSockets-based chat client and server.

item.title: Writing a NodeJS webserver
item.description: An exploration into building an HTTP server in NodeJS, and a look into what a webserver is, anyway.

item.title: Transforming numbers
item.description: A little numerical diversion, changing things to other things.

item.title: Writing a jQuery plugin to create scrolling regions in HTML
item.description: How to make a scrolling pane in HTML/CSS; in particular, how to wrap it all in a jQuery plugin for easy reuse.

item.title: HTML canvas scratcher revisited
item.description: An exercise in refactoring code from being closely tied to a page to being less closely tied. This is based on the previous scratcher code.

item.title: Computing π with Chudnovsky and GMP
item.description: Crank out as many digits of π as you desire! Limited only by your imagination! Crank out your own modest π in the browser, too! (Limited by 64-bit IEEE-754.)

item.title: Object Pools
item.description: Featuring goats!

item.title: Image Processing Convolutions
item.description: How to use convolution matrices for common image filters.

item.title: Drag and Drop
item.description: Learn how it's done, and code your own.

item.title: m4 Macros and CSS
item.description: Use the unholy m4 macro processor with CSS. Or not.

item.title: HTML canvas <tt>globalCompositeOperation</tt>
item.description: Simulate a scratcher or scratch-off in-browser using HTML.

item.title: Parallel Programming with OpenMP
item.description: Intro that includes a link to my OpenMP-enabled Mandelbrot set generator.

item.title: The Mandelbrot Set
item.description: Includes cheesy little online explorer app.

item.title: Minimax
item.description: Lookahead AI, just like what powers the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WOPR">WOPR<a>

item.title: Adobe AIR
item.description: Very very short getting-started document.

item.title: HTML5 Canvas Part II: Pixel Manipulation
Function Pointers and Cooperative Multitasking
item.description: Just like Grandma used to multitask.

item.title: Fuzz Testing
item.description: Super-Superrrrr... oh forget it.

item.title: Digital Sound
item.description: Generate some waveforms!

item.title: Pointers in C Part I: Basics
item.description: It's really not that bad.

item.title: The Observer Pattern
item.description: Things happen. Your code observes them. What could be more exciting?

item.title: The Monte Carlo Method for Game AI
item.description: Beat your computer at Connect-4.

item.title: Getting Started with Flash Development
item.description: You can do it with free tools on the command line.

item.title: Arch vs. Slackware, a friendly comparison
Parsing XML Streams with SAX
item.description: No need to parse that whole DOM—just handle tags as they show up.

item.title: Of Thunderbird and Usenet
item.description: For old NNTP froods.
